tex:
(a) A shop sells dress fabric for \$2.97 per metre.

(i) (b) A customer buys 9 metres of this fabric.

Calculate the change he receives from \$50.

\$ 

(c) The selling price of \$2.97 per metre is an increase of 8\% on the cost price.

Calculate the cost price.

\$  per metre

(d) A dressmaker charges \$35 or 2300 rupees to make a dress.

Calculate the difference in price when the exchange rate is 1 rupee = \$0.0153.
Give your answer in rupees.

 rupees

(e) The dressmaker measures a length of fabric as 600 m, correct to the nearest 5 metres.
He cuts this into dress lengths of 9 m, correct to the nearest metre.

Calculate the largest number of complete dress lengths he could cut.

(a) Make $t$ the subject of the formula $s = k - t^2$.

(b) Factorise $x^2 - 25$.

(c) Simplify $\dfrac{x^2 - 25}{x^2 - 2x - 35}$.

(d) Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.

$\dfrac{x-8}{x} + \dfrac{3x}{x+1}$

(e) Find the integer values of $n$ that satisfy the inequality.

$18 - 2n \leq 6 \leq 18 - 2n + 12$

texHtml:
The frequency table shows information about the time, $m$ minutes, that each of 160 people spend in a library.
Time ($m$ minutes)$0 \leq m < 10$$10 \leq m < 40$$40 \leq m < 60$$60 \leq m < 90$$90 \leq m < 100$$100 \leq m < 120$
Frequency3394355119
(a) Find the probability that one of these people, chosen at random, spends more than 100 minutes in the library. (b) Calculate an estimate of the mean time spent in the library. minutes (c) Complete the cumulative frequency table below.
Time ($m$ minutes)$m < 10$$m < 40$$m < 60$$m < 90$$m < 100$$m < 120$
Cumulative frequency342
(d) On the grid opposite, draw the cumulative frequency diagram. \IfFileExists{\currfiledir Figures/Q07-CumulativeFrequency.png}{ }{ \fbox{ box{0.7\textwidth}{[Q07-CumulativeFrequency.png]}} } (e) Use your cumulative frequency diagram to find (i) (f) the median, minutes (g) the interquartile range, minutes (h) the 90th percentile, minutes (i) the number of people who spend more than 30 minutes in the library.

tex:
(a) Find the magnitude of the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 7 \end{pmatrix}$.

(b) The determinant of the matrix $\begin{pmatrix} 6 & 2m \\ 5 & m \end{pmatrix}$ is 24.

Find the value of $m$.

(c) $L = \begin{pmatrix} 2 & 5 \end{pmatrix}$, $M = \begin{pmatrix} -4 \\ 2 \end{pmatrix}$, $N = \begin{pmatrix} 3 & 9 \end{pmatrix}$.

Work out the following.

(i) (d) $NM$

(e) $LM$

(f) $L^2$

(g) $L^{-1}$

The table shows the first five terms of sequences A, B and C.

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Sequence & 1st term & 2nd term & 3rd term & 4th term & 5th term & 6th term \\
\hline
A & 0 & 1 & 4 & 9 & 16 & \\
\hline
B & 4 & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 & \\
\hline
C & -4 & -4 & -2 & 2 & 8 & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(a) Complete the table.

(b) Find an expression for the nth term of

(i) (c) sequence A,

(d) sequence B.

(e) Find the value of $n$ when the nth term of sequence A is 576.

(f) Find an expression for the nth term of sequence C.
Give your answer in its simplest form.

(i) (g) Expression:

(h) Find the value of the 30th term of sequence C.
